answer:
**Chapter 41 of the *Viṣṇu Smṛti* addresses "defiling acts" (*malāvaha*), a category of impure or contaminating behaviors.**[^1]

It proscribes three specific actions:
- Killing birds (*pakṣiṇām*), aquatic animals (*jalacarāṇām*), and those born in water (*jalajānām*).[^2]
- Killing worms and insects.[^3]
- Eating food associated with liquor.[^4]

**These are collectively deemed "defiling acts" (*malāvaha*), implying ritual impurity.** The central prescriptive rule mandates purification through one of two penances (*prāyaścitta*): the *taptakṛcchra* (heated austerity) or the *kṛcchrātikṛcchra* (intensified austerity).[^5]

[^1]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 41, [paragraph](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/41/translation/#p-572d18).
[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 41, [paragraph](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/41/translation/#p-17a394).
[^3]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 41, [paragraph](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/41/translation/#p-ef13c5).
[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 41, [paragraph](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/41/translation/#p-bbb63f).
[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 41, [paragraph](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/41/translation/#p-8818cd).
